title: Robust Smoky Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Egyptian stew made with lamb, hot smoked paprika, and a blend of spices, resulting in a jammy and springy texture, perfect for a monsoon evening. This dish is gluten-free friendly and features the bold flavors of Egypt.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the hot smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender and falling apart.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the lamb tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
